D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

LPI vs Tone Levels

a finer screen ruling resolves more detail, but at a fixed device resolution it buys that detail by throwing away grey levels

Each halftone dot is built from a cluster of tiny device pixels. A dot cell spans DPI/LPI device dots on a side, so the number of grey levels it can render is (DPI/LPI)² + 1. Raise the screen ruling (LPI) and the dots get finer — but each cell now holds fewer device pixels, so fewer tones survive.

Slide the screen ruling and switch the device resolution. The readout tracks the available grey levels; the ramp above shows that quantisation, and the screened ramp below shows the matching dot grid.
